title: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind
display_title: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind
wikitext:
[]: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by TheCarkolum » Thu Jun 29, 2017 11:09 am Imagine you are in Shinji's shoes. Rei gives you the chance to abort or continue the Instrumentality. What would you do? Do you think Instrumentality would fill the gaps in our mind/heart and could give us free will as individuals forming a single conscience, or do you think there's still hope for humanity to understand each other? I'm assuming, of course, that aborting Instrumentality would turn the things back to the normal world, not red earth or returning souls or shit. Just the regular world. I... I'm not sure if I am totally honest. Im so curious about how would a global conscience be like. But, at the same time, no one wants to lose his inherent being. I think that Instrumentality is a coward way out, and eventually nothing will be solved, because nothing would be technically existing. It would be like death, in a conscious level. On the other hand, mankind will have to keep fighting if it wants to understand itself. We are FAR, very far from being able to understand ourselves. And I think we're not even on the right road. So... the options are: returning to nothing or keep waiting for a distant hope. So, what do you think? Anybody would go ahead with Instrumentality? Anyone would be OK with that?

[]: Re: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by The Cruel » Thu Jun 29, 2017 12:52 pm Short answer: No!!! Long answer: Why would I want a cowards way out into a fake Garden Eden, if I rather want something true and real than just something out of my imaginagion? I can accept myself, the circumstances, the people around me and the world I live in and these are enough reasons to be against Instrumantality. That's only a means to play god and no one asked for giving up their own identity, becoming goo and having their consciousness floating in nothingness. Otherwise you're nuts or suicidial to want that.

[]: Re: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Jun 29, 2017 1:08 pm Short answer: No; long answer: Absolutely No!

[]: Re: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by silvermoonlight » Thu Jun 29, 2017 1:18 pm No because I don't like the idea of being interconnected with others and seeing there deepest and darkest thoughts and desires as I think some thoughts and desires are private for a reason and no one should ever be forced to have to show them to everyone else. Not to mention that Human Instrumentality doesn't talk about being forced to share your minds with that of killers, murders and other criminals whose mental images would not be nice viewing on any level. Hence why if someone put me in it I would want out as fast as possible...and wouldn't start it if it was offered to me.

[]: Re: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by Stillborn » Thu Jun 29, 2017 1:33 pm If Nemz was still with us, he would probably vote for. Me... I don't want to be connected to mind of certain people XD Besides I know funnir ways for transhumanism, like connecting to Netsphere, or joining the Great Old Ones...

[]: Re: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jun 29, 2017 1:52 pm While we haven't had this exact question before, we have had similar ones. And I think it's safe to say that NemZ would have and I would both vote for Gendou's Techno-Rapture as shown the in TV series. The rather different deal on offer in EoE is another matter, coming as it does in a lose-lose bundle (even if we finesse Shinji's massive dummy-spit early on in the proceedings, the ecological effects of these tsunami in the rejection choice would lead to, well, matters rehashed at length a decade and more ago) really requires the answer "none of the above", and the James T. Kirk solution to the Kobayashi Maru exercise.

[]: Re: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by C.T.1290 » Thu Jun 29, 2017 2:22 pm I don't think so. I rather be my own person and retain my own individuality, I think I'm fine just the way I am. As for others to get along and come to an understanding, that is something we all have to work for to achieve. Sometimes we have to take the long road towards that instead of just taking the shortcut. Instrumentality was just someone's way of cheating everyone of their own selves, depraving them of their rights to be. And turning into goo is one terrible way to go. Everyone's about as fine as they are. If they want to change, they have to work towards it instead of the easy way.

[]: Re: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Jun 29, 2017 2:30 pm Instrumentality, for all mankind?? Nope. Instrumentality for me and a select few? Yes please. Let us transcend as one into the vessel that is Eva, and become an all-powerful god. Damn the unworthy to a cold, and tangy oblivion.

[]: Re: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 29, 2017 2:48 pm A transhumanist scenario of my own design? Don't mind if I do! The one depicted in NGE? Just... no.

[]: Re: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Jun 29, 2017 6:14 pm I would enjoy surviving ego death to enslave all your souls in the future that is Chuckman.

[]: Re: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by FrDougal9000 » Thu Jun 29, 2017 6:14 pm I have a feeling that if all of mankind were to come together into a collective hive mind, it would be as if everyone was on Twitter and constantly tweeting everything that came to mind, with no way of blocking it out and everyone follows each other. In short, it would be hell. Regardless, as someone who holds the freedom of the individual as a personally important value, I'd rather not sacrifice that for the idea of 'getting along'.

[]: Re: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Jun 29, 2017 6:24 pm Really it depends on whether we're individuals glued together or we combine into a divine being.

[]: Re: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by Joseki » Thu Jun 29, 2017 6:38 pm I honestly like food, sport and girls too much to give up on them. Then there are smaller things like the smell of the streets after a rainy day, a beautiful clear sky of a sunny day, a walk in the park with my sweet lady... What Instrumentality offers isn't worth what I'll lose for it.

[]: Re: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by Cybermat47 » Thu Jun 29, 2017 7:04 pm No. I'd prefer not to run away from reality.

[]: Re: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 29, 2017 7:21 pm Chuckman wrote: Really it depends on whether we're individuals glued together or we combine into a divine being.

[]: Re: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by Stillborn » Thu Jun 29, 2017 8:10 pm I believe they succeded and this entity is now writing down his gestalt memories as fanfics on FFnet D

[]: Re: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by TheCarkolum » Fri Jun 30, 2017 10:34 am FrDougal9000 wrote: I have a feeling that if all of mankind were to come together into a collective hive mind, it would be as if everyone was on Twitter and constantly tweeting everything that came to mind, with no way of blocking it out and everyone follows each other. In short, it would be hell.

[]: Re: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by The Cruel » Fri Jun 30, 2017 12:04 pm TheCarkolum wrote: Oh cmon, you gotta admit it would be such an experience (if you could get out eventually...). Imagine. Everybody would agree in the Evageeks Forum...

[]: Re: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Fri Jun 30, 2017 12:06 pm ^ Exactly! If you could have EVERYTHING you wanted with the blink of an eye....would you even bother getting up in the morning?

[]: Re: How many people in this forum would support an Instrumentality for mankind?             Quote Post by The Cruel » Fri Jun 30, 2017 12:25 pm xanderkh wrote: ^ Exactly! If you could have EVERYTHING you wanted with the blink of an eye....would you even bother getting up in the morning?
